message:
perf(bench): update widget benchmarks for new scoring algorithm

Updates the widget benchmark suite to cover the enhanced command palette
fuzzy scoring algorithm and measure performance across various scenarios.

New benchmarks:

command_palette/score_single:
- Measures single candidate scoring latency
- Tests short, medium, and long candidate strings
- Validates sub-microsecond performance for interactive use

command_palette/score_batch:
- Batch scoring for 100, 500, and 1000 candidates
- Measures throughput for real-world command sets
- Validates acceptable latency for keystroke response

command_palette/match_ranking:
- End-to-end ranking including sort
- Tests ranking stability across query variations
- Measures memory allocation patterns

Updated baselines:
- Adjusted expected throughput for new algorithm complexity
- Added regression thresholds for CI integration
- Updated comparison baselines for dirty-row optimization

Run benchmarks with:
  cargo bench -p ftui-widgets --bench widget_bench

Performance targets:
- Single score: <500ns average
- Batch 1000: <5ms total
- Interactive (100 candidates): <1ms with ranking

These benchmarks ensure the enhanced scoring algorithm maintains
acceptable performance for interactive command palette use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
